english: sounding words, pompous style; *lág-len tón-po* (lag len ton po) W. highly skilled, well practised. — mton-mtiṅ (mthon mthing) 'the high blue (thing)' viz. the hair of the head of Buddha, always represented as of a light sky-blue.

to confess, to avow, nyés-pa (nyes pa) Dzl.; mtól tsáṅs (mthol tshangs) (cf. gyod-tsáṅs (gyod tshangs)) confession, acknowledgment, mtól-tsáṅs byéd-pa (mthol tshangs byed pa) Dzl., mtol bsags-pa (mthol bsags pa) to make confession, to confess, which acc. to Buddhist doctrine involves atonement and remission of sins.
1. Ld. high, elevated, *tíṅ-si saṅ tos ma len* (thing si sang thos ma len) do not take more than is right! — 2. Mil.?

pf. btags (btags), fut. btag (btag), imp. tog (thog), 1. to grind, raṅ-tág-gis (rang thag gis) in a mill Dzl., gro (gro) wheat, pyé-mar (phye mar) to flour: to reduce to powder, to pulverize, by means of two stones (cf. gtun (gtun)); to mash. — 2. to weave, snám-bu (snam bu) cloth; ˳tág(-pa)-po ('thag (-pa) po), ˳tág-mkan ('thag mkhan) a weaver; dar-tag-bú-mo (dar thag bu mo) the daughter of a silk-weaver Glr. — tag-stáṅ (thag stang) loom Sch. — ˳tag-rdó ('thag rdo) mill-stone, grinding-stone(?) Sch.
Wdṅ. a bodily defect or deformity, prob. teṅ-po (theng po).
liking, pleasure; will; joy, v. the following article.

I. 1. to be pleasant, agreeable, well-pleasing ccdp., gsuṅ de kun śin-tu séms-la ˳tád-pa źig byuṅ (gsung de kun shin tu sems la 'thad pa zhig byung) all these sayings have pleased me very much Mil. — 2. (not governing a case) to please, to be acceptable, to be considered as good, to be (generally) admitted, mi ˳tád-par mtoṅ (mi 'thad par mthong) I see that (this reading) is not generally accepted Zam.; źes-paaṅ ˳tád-do (zhes pa'ang 'thad do) it occurs also in this form Zam.; mi-˳tád-de (mi 'thad de) wrong! Was. (294); to be fit, proper, suitable (syn. to ˳os-pa ('os pa)), sems zér-ba mi ˳tád-la (sems zer ba mi 'thad la) as it is not proper to call it soul, as it cannot fitly be called soul Mil. — 3. a familiar word, very frq. used, in W. almost the only word for dgá-ba (dga' ba) and ˳dód-pa ('dod pa), *sem tád-de* (sems thad de) cheerfully, joyfully W., ˳tád-rgyu méd-pa tsam źig-la ('thad rgyu med pa tsam zhig la) prob.: as he became angry Mil.; *sém-mi náṅ-ne tad soṅ* (sems mi nang ne thad song), also *tíṅ* (thing) (q. v.) *túg-pa-ne tad-soṅ* (thug pa ne thad song) W. I have been heartily glad; ˳tád-˳tád-dra yaṅ ('thad 'thad dra yang) Mil. though apparently rejoicing; *mā tad-tád* (ma thad thad) W. I am very glad of that; *sem tád cug-cè* (sems thad cug ce) W. to make glad, to exhilarate; *sú-heb-bi źó-la mi tád-da* (su heb bi zho la mi thad da) W. does your honour not like curdled milk? *tád-kaṅ* (thad khang) W. willing, ready; *gá-ru tád-na soṅ* (ga ru thad na song) W. go wherever you like; lóg-pa-˳tad (log pa 'thad) let us turn back Glr.; ráṅ-ṅi ˳tád-la (rang ngi 'thad la) voluntarily, spontaneously. II Sch. = ˳táṅ-pa ('thang pa), ˳tad-ldáṅ ('thad ldang) = ˳táṅ-po ('thang po).
(cog. to brtán-po (brtan po) and tán-po! (than po!)) Cs. also ˳tád-pa ('thad pa), firmness, constancy, in Lexx. explained by nán-tan (nan tan): mi ˳tán-po (mi 'than po) a steady, resolute man Cs.

to combat, to fight, in a battle: to quarrel, to dispute, to brawl; ka-tsúb daṅ ˳táb-pa (kha tshub dang 'thab pa) to struggle with a snow-storm Mil.; ˳táb-pa méd-ciṅ śi-ba ('thab pa med cing shi ba) to die peaceably, without a struggle; ˳táb-pa dúm-na ('thab pa dum na) when quarreling (persons) are reconciled; ka-˳táb (kha 'thab) Cs. a fighting with the mouth, altercation; lag-˳táb (lag 'thab) Cs. a fighting with one's hands, a close fighting, a scuffle (Sch. gesticulation?); ˳tab-król ('thab khrol) Lex. dispute, contest; ˳tab-krúg ('thab khrug) prob. id.; (Lex. आयुध weapon?); *tab-ḍhäb* (thab dhab) C. weapons, arms; ˳tab-cás ('thab chas) ammunition, requisites for war Schr.; ˳tab-brdúṅs ('thab brdungs), á-kuí ˳tab-brdúṅs (a khu'i 'thab brdungs) the quarreling and thrashing of my uncle Mil.; ˳táb-mo ('thab mo) quarrel, fight, row, fray, battle, B. and col. frq., ˳táb-mo byéd-pa ('thab mo byed pa) B., *tó-cè* (tho ce) W., to quarrel, fight etc.; ˳táb-mó spród-pa ('thab mo sprod pa) to fight a battle, to join battle Glr.; ˳tab-źób ('thab zhob) a dry cough Sch. — ˳táb-rtsód ('thab rtsod) altercation, quarrel, brawl, frq. — ˳tab-ya ('thab ya) antagonist, kyód-kyis ṅai ˳táb-ya byed dgos (khyod kyis ngai 'thab ya byed dgos) thou must contend with me Glr. — bdúd-moi ˳táb-ya (bdud mo'i 'thab ya) a termagant, a she-devil to struggle with Mil.; gnas-skabs-kyi ˳táb-ya (gnas skabs kyi 'thab ya) the antagonists of life, i. e. the family and relations a secular man has to struggle with Mil. — ˳tab-rágs ('thab rags) intrenchment, breast-work, fortification (.
